zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard de Kirkbride II於大約1266年出生在英國英格蘭恩布利亞吉可布利德時，他的父親Richard De Kirkbride48歲，母親Lady Euphemia de Livington37歲。他和Christine De Wigton至少有2個兒子。他去世於1330年在英國英格蘭波克夏溫莎女爵 卡素享年65歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭波克夏溫莎女爵 卡素。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Kirkbride
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English (Cumbria): habitational name from a place in Cumbria near Carlisle, named from Old Norse kirkja ‘church’ + the Irish saint's name Bride (see Kilbride ).

variants.name: Kilbride
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Kieran, Aidan.

Irish (Connacht) and Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Brighde (Irish) or Mac Gille Brighde (Scottish) ‘son of the servant of (Saint) Brigit’, also often shortened and Anglicized as McBride . The name Brigit means ‘exalted’. Brigit was originally a pagan goddess, many of whose attributes, such as a sacred fire, became attached to the historical figure of Saint Brigit of Kildare (452–523), founder of the first Irish convent.

Scottish: habitational name from any of various places with this name, from Gaelic Cill Bríghde ‘church of Saint Brigit’ (cill being from Latin cella ‘room, cell’), for example Kilbride in Lanarkshire.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard de Kirkbride II was born about 1266, in Kirkbride, Cumbria, England, United Kingdom, his father, Richard De Kirkbride, was 48 and his mother, Lady Euphemia de Livington, was 37. He had at least 2 sons with Christine De Wigton. He died in 1330, in Windsor Castle, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 65, and was buried in Windsor Castle, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard de Kirkbride II est né environ 1266, à Kirkbride, Cumbria, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard De Kirkbride, avait 48 ans et sa mère, Lady Euphemia de Livington, avait 37 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Christine De Wigton. Il est décédé en 1330, à Windsor Castle, Berkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterré à Windsor Castle, Berkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Er hatte mit Christine De Wigton mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1330 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Windsor Castle, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Windsor Castle, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard de Kirkbride IIが約1266年にイギリスイギリスクウンブリアキルクブリドで生まれた時，父Richard De Kirkbrideは48歳，母Lady Euphemia de Livingtonは37歳であった。彼はChristine De Wigtonとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼は1330年にイギリスイギリスバークシュアイアウィンドソル カストルで65歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスバークシュアイアウィンドソル カストルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard de Kirkbride II(이)가 대략 1266년 영국 잉글랜드 c브리아 커k브라이드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard De Kirkbride(은)는 48세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Euphemia de Livington(은)는 37세였다. 그는 Christine De Wigton(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1330년 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르 윈드소르 카스틀에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르 윈드소르 카스틀에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard de Kirkbride II родился около 1266 г. в местности киркбрид, кумбря, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Richard De Kirkbride было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Euphemia de Livington, 37. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына от Christine De Wigton. Он умер в 1330 г. в местности виндзор кастл, биркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности виндзор кастл, биркшир, Англия, Великобритания.
